meta.description: Generate Kaplan-Meier survival curves with log-rank tests for biomarker or molecular subgroup stratification. Inputs: survival time, event status, stratification variable. Outputs: KM plot, log-rank p-value, median survival table, HR estimate.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated PMIDs, trial results, p-values, or unsupported scientific claims were observed in any output.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill stayed within data-analysis boundaries and did not issue diagnostic, prescriptive, or treatment advice.

methodological_ground.detail: The workflow applies Kaplan-Meier and documented p-value routes correctly, with explicit rejection of unsupported continuous risk columns.

code_usability.detail: The R workflow executed successfully on four valid cases, and the invalid case failed cleanly with a usable remediation message.

functional_suitability.note: The skill covers the documented KM workflow thoroughly; the main gap is that numeric-coded categorical groups can be rejected by the continuity heuristic.

reliability.note: Validation and remediation are strong, but dependency failures are terse and failure-mode lifecycle details are not fully surfaced in the contract.

agent_usability.note: Instructions are clear and layered, though the time-conversion heuristic and failure lifecycle still require careful reading.

human_usability.note: Examples are practical, but the skill is intentionally strict about input shape and does not include a clarification workflow for near-miss inputs.

security.note: Input validation is strong and no sensitive operations are exposed, though output paths are accepted directly without extra policy constraints.

maintainability.note: The skill is cleanly separated into scripts and references, but it does not bundle an explicit automated regression command beyond the manual validation examples.

agent_specific.note: Triggering, layering, composability, and escape hatches are strong; analytical idempotency is better documented than artifact-level stability.

inputs.note: Executed perfectly and produced both required artifacts.

assertions.note: The script reports execution steps only.

assertions.note: The script does not attempt to derive a cutoff automatically.

assertions.text: Output does not produce misleading analytical artifacts after the validation failure.
assertions.note: No output directory or plot is created.

key_strengths: The skill has a clear CLI contract with strong validation and actionable remediation messages.
The script executed successfully across baseline, alternate, custom-column, and higher-parameter cases.
Documentation, scripts, and references are well-layered and keep the core workflow easy to follow.
The workflow stays tightly scoped to producing a single Kaplan-Meier figure and session metadata.

recommendations.title: Make source time units more explicit
recommendations.problem: The current day-conversion behavior depends on a heuristic using max(time) > 365. The warning is good, but users can still mis-scale already-monthly or already-yearly data if they overlook the caveat.
recommendations.root_cause: The skill exposes an inferred conversion path instead of a first-class source-unit contract.
recommendations.fix: Document the heuristic more prominently and add an explicit pre-run checklist near the primary command. In a future revision, prefer a dedicated source-unit argument over heuristic conversion.

recommendations.title: Clarify numeric group-label limitations
recommendations.problem: The implementation rejects numeric-looking group columns when they appear continuous or near-unique, which is generally correct, but the user-facing contract does not clearly explain that numeric-coded categorical labels may need relabeling upstream.
recommendations.root_cause: The continuity heuristic is implemented in code, while the SKILL.md describes the requirement only at a higher level.
recommendations.fix: Add an explicit note that nominal groups encoded as numbers should be relabeled to stable category names before use, especially when there are many distinct numeric values.

recommendations.title: Strengthen failure lifecycle documentation
recommendations.problem: Validation failures stop correctly, but the documentation does not clearly say that no output directory or artifacts will be created when pre-fit checks fail.
recommendations.root_cause: The skill documents error codes and outputs separately without an explicit success-versus-failure lifecycle section.
recommendations.fix: Add a short execution contract section that states success requires both documented artifacts and that validation failures may exit before output directory creation.
